questions.prompt: A customer's domain already has an SPF TXT record from a CRM that sends marketing email. You're now setting up Microsoft 365. What's the right move?
choices: Replace the existing SPF record with the M365 one
Add a second SPF TXT record alongside the existing one
Merge the two into a single SPF record that includes both senders
Delete the existing record and start over
questions.explanation: Only one v=spf1 record per domain is allowed. Two SPF records make SPF fail for every sender. Merge into one record that 'includes' both the M365 SPF and the CRM SPF, plus any direct IPs the customer needs.

questions.prompt: After adding the DKIM CNAMEs for an M365 tenant, mail still arrives unsigned. What did you most likely miss?
choices: DKIM signing must be enabled per domain in the M365 admin centre
DKIM CNAMEs need to be at the apex of the zone
DKIM requires DMARC to be set to p=reject first
DKIM only signs internal mail; outbound to external domains is unsigned
questions.explanation: Adding the CNAME pair publishes the public keys, but M365 doesn't start signing outbound mail until DKIM signing is toggled on per-domain in the admin centre under Email & collaboration → Threat policies → DKIM.
